content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
note: Finished implementing dynamic default resolution: (1) Option.__init__ validates dict-based default specs right after super().__init__ (TypeError for non-string keys / non-dict values), (2) Option.get_default returns None for dict defaults so type casting doesn't choke on the unresolved dict, (3) Option.get_help_extra shows '[default: (dynamic)]' when show_default is set, (4) added Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults(ctx), called from Command.parse_args right after the main param loop - it resolves dict defaults against other options' final values (matched by '--opt-name' in p.opts/secondary_opts), only when the target's ParameterSource is DEFAULT, with later dict entries taking precedence. All 739 existing tests pass, verified basic/multi-dep/envvar/help-text/validation scenarios manually. Did not touch to_info_dict, type_cast_value, or process_value. types.py untouched. Diff written to patch.txt in my own environment.
